description: This site offers vibrant coral gardens and dramatic drop-offs teeming with life. Expect strong currents that attract pelagics like barracuda and reef sharks. The steep walls are covered in soft corals, with overhangs and swim-throughs to explore. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, making it ideal for photography. The site suits advanced divers due to occasional surge and depth. Night dives reveal nocturnal creatures like octopus and lobster. Nearby pinnacles host schools of anthias and butterflyfish. The warm, clear waters make it a year-round destination.

name: Shaab Abu Nugar
region: Africa, Egypt, Red Sea Governorate, Qesm Hurghada
description: This horseshoe-shaped reef sits in the Red Sea near Hurghada. Strong currents sweep the outer walls, attracting pelagic species like barracuda and reef sharks. Inside, sheltered lagoons host vibrant soft corals and small reef fish. The site drops to 30 meters, offering dramatic wall dives with swim-throughs. Ideal for drift diving, it's best suited for intermediate to advanced divers. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, showcasing the rich marine ecosystem. The reef top peaks near the surface, allowing deep or shallow exploration. Night dives reveal nocturnal creatures among the coral formations.

name: El Fanadir Dacht
region: Africa, Egypt, Red Sea Governorate, Qesm Hurghada
description: El Fanadir Dacht offers a stunning wall dive with vibrant coral gardens teeming with anthias, lionfish, and occasional reef sharks. The site features dramatic drop-offs and swim-throughs, with excellent visibility year-round. Gentle currents attract schools of barracuda and trevally. Ideal for all skill levels, with depths ranging from 10 to 40 meters. The hard and soft coral formations create a colorful underwater landscape, perfect for photographers. Warm water temperatures make it comfortable for extended dives.

name: Erg Hornkorab
region: Indian Ocean, Red Sea
description: This dive site in the Red Sea features dramatic underwater mountain peaks and steep drop-offs, creating a thrilling wall dive. Vibrant soft corals blanket the slopes, attracting schools of fusiliers and anthias. Look for reef sharks patrolling the blue, while eagle rays glide along the current. The site offers excellent visibility and moderate currents, ideal for drift diving. Depth ranges from 15-meter plateaus to over 40 meters along the walls. Occasional sightings of hammerheads add excitement for advanced divers. The topography provides shelter for moray eels and lionfish in crevices.

name: El Sayej
region: Africa, Egypt, Red Sea Governorate, Qesm Hurghada
description: This vibrant reef sits near Hurghada, teeming with colorful soft corals and schools of anthias. The sloping wall drops to 30 meters, offering encounters with turtles, reef sharks, and occasional eagle rays. Strong currents attract pelagics, making it ideal for drift diving. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, with warm water year-round. The site's healthy coral formations and diverse marine life create an unforgettable underwater spectacle.
